{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 3, "RX.1": 2, "RX.3": 4, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The feedback identifies a gap in the comparison to other methods but doesn't explicitly link it back to evidence.", "R1.4": "Names prior work (Rosenbaum and Rubin) but doesn't compare to 3-5 recent works or articulate sharp deltas.", "RX.1": "Doesn't explicitly state uncertainty for any claims, even regarding the limitations of the proposed method.", "RX.3": "Mostly within scope, focusing on causal inference from observational data. The suggestion to add a comparison to modern baselines is a minor drift.", "RX.4": "No regressions identified; the feedback focuses on constructive improvements rather than harmful content.", "RX.5": "Provides high-level suggestions for improvement but doesn't trace specific changes or sources."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.8, "R1.4": 0.7, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The feedback identifies a gap in the comparison to other methods but doesn't explicitly link it back to evidence.
rationale.R1.4: Names prior work (Rosenbaum and Rubin) but doesn't compare to 3-5 recent works or articulate sharp deltas.
rationale.RX.1: Doesn't explicitly state uncertainty for any claims, even regarding the limitations of the proposed method.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope, focusing on causal inference from observational data. The suggestion to add a comparison to modern baselines is a minor drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions identified; the feedback focuses on constructive improvements rather than harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: Provides high-level suggestions for improvement but doesn't trace specific changes or sources.
